seoTitle: Mares Run vs Marks Point — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mares Run and Marks Point. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mares Run vs Marks Point: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mares Run and Marks Point.
heading: Mares Run vs Marks Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mares Run, New South Wales and Marks Point, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Marks Point has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Mares Run (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mares Run or Marks Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mares Run has a flood risk score of 53/100, while Marks Point scores 28/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mares Run scores 43/100 for bushfire risk. Marks Point scores 19/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mares Run or Marks Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Marks Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.75% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
